description: A period of approximately four weeks; originally based on the moon's cycle. 'Lunoa' names the monthly rhythm—the moon's complete journey from new to full to new again.
sound_symbolism: The word follows the moon. 'Lu' begins with flowing change, cycle starting. 'No' grounds at each phase. Finally 'a' opens to completion, month fulfilled.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'ta lunoa' (one month). Related to 'lunei' (moon) through shared root sense. The intermediate unit between week and year.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Months followed the moon—the visible cycle in the sky that marked time for those without calendars.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Monthly rhythms invite reflection—each cycle an opportunity to notice change and impermanence.
